title: Improve file preview for wide Excel files
excerpt:
Current behavior

posts.author: gwentsai (Gwenn)
posts.cookedHtml:
Current behavior

When previewing Excel files in monday, if the file layout is wide (e.g., designed to fit one A4 page), the preview automatically splits the content into multiple pages. This causes the preview layout to appear different from the original file.

Additionally, monday currently does not support automatically converting uploaded files (e.g., Excel) into PDF format.

Use case

Some of our clients upload formatted Excel documents (reports, forms, or approval sheets) that are designed to fit a single A4 page. When users open the file preview in monday, the layout becomes fragmented due to automatic pagination, which makes it difficult to review the document quickly.

Since many users rely on the preview feature for quick checks rather than downloading files, maintaining the original layout is important.

Suggested improvement

  1. Provide an option to preserve the original page layout when previewing Excel files (especially A4 formatted documents).

  2. Allow users to auto-convert uploaded Excel files to PDF so that the preview displays the document exactly as intended.

  3. Alternatively, provide a “Preview as PDF” rendering option in the file preview.

This would significantly improve the usability of file previews for formatted documents and reporting files.

posts.author: Iryna_Belaya (Iryna Belaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @gwentsai ,

You can try Smart Spreadsheet for this goal. It can be added as an Object, which is especially convenient for reading massive spreadsheet files.

image1805×1093 558 KB

But if you need to read them in a view, you can upload files to a Smart Spreadsheet view
